channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Governor Dauda Lawal Honoured for Zamfara Infrastructure Achievements
item.description: For the second edition of the Broadcasting Organisations of Nigeria (BON) Awards wey hold for Abuja, Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don collect the Infrastructure Governor of the Year award. Th...

item.title: Rory McIlroy Returns to Defend Masters Title Amid Strong Field
item.description: Rory McIlroy returns to Augusta as the defending Masters champion, aiming to make history by attempting a consecutive win a year after completing his career Grand Slam. At 36, McIlroy feels liberated ...

item.title: President Suluhu directs government to reduce fuel consumption and streamline official travel
item.description: Tanzania President Samia Suluhu Hassan has instructed government offices to immediately reduce fuel use amid global energy disruptions causing higher prices. Speaking at a swearing-in ceremony on Apri...

item.title: Economic Divergence: Woolworths Gains While Bunnings Struggles Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Recent weeks have seen a surprising divergence in the performance of Australia’s two largest retailers, Woolworths and Bunnings. While Australia grapples with ongoing uncertainty fuelled by the Iran w...

item.title: Divided Opinions on Ukraine’s Conscription Challenges and Draft Evasion
item.description: Independent readers have expressed sharply contrasting views on Ukraine’s current conscription crisis, which has emerged amidst rising draft dodging and increased pressure on the armed forces. Reporti...

item.title: Rory McIlroy and Shane Lowry Attend the 2026 Masters with Their Families
item.description: Rory McIlroy and Shane Lowry returned to Augusta National Golf Club for the 2026 Masters, but the spotlight shifted from the golfers to their children. Rory's five-year-old daughter, Poppy, joined the...

item.title: Israeli Military Offensive in Lebanon Results in Over 250 Deaths and Numerous Injuries
item.description: Israel launched its most intense airstrikes on Lebanon to date, resulting in at least 254 fatalities and more than 1,160 injuries, according to Lebanon's civil defence authorities. The attacks occurre...

item.title: Province provides $6.75 million to help four B.C. companies expand and innovate
item.description: Four British Columbia companies will collectively receive $6.75 million in provincial funding to support expansion and innovation projects aimed at strengthening the province’s economy and promoting s...

item.title: Karnataka 2nd PUC Result 2026 Not Accessible: NDTV Server Access Denied
item.description: The much-awaited Karnataka Second PUC result for the year 2026 has been officially published online, but many students are facing difficulties accessing it through the NDTV website. When attempting to...

item.title: Supreme Court ends SEC shutdown case against Rappler after eight years
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has officially put an end to the legal battle between Rappler and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), closing one of the country’s most high-profile medi...

item.title: QC Robbery-Holdup: Four Apprehended, Actor-Vlogger Under Investigation
item.description: Apat na tao ang naaresto ng Quezon City Police Department (QCPD) kaugnay ng isang robbery-holdup incident na nangyari sa Quezon City. Ayon sa ulat, ang mga suspect ay hinala na sangkot sa panghoholdap...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict De-escalates After Pakistan-Brokered Ceasefire
item.description: A fragile ceasefire has been reached between the United States and Iran following intense negotiations mediated by Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and the country’s Chief of Defence Forces (C...

item.title: Allahabad High Court upholds proceedings against UP Muslim Personal Law Board chief over remarks alleging bias in BJP-ruled states
item.description: The Allahabad High Court has rejected a plea filed by Noor Ahmed Ajahri, chief of the Uttar Pradesh Muslim Personal Law Board, seeking to quash criminal proceedings against him. Ajahri faces charges f...

item.title: Kenya Champions Climate Integration in Corporate Governance with Chapter Zero Kenya Launch
item.description: The Kenyan government is really pushing companies to make climate action and sustainability a core part of how they do business. This follows the launch of Chapter Zero Kenya, a new initiative aimed a...

item.title: Rajmohan Gandhi presents a benevolent vision for Hindus in his latest book
item.description: In his new book, *Do You Know Your Hinduism?: Notes For Modern-Day Hindus*, Rajmohan Gandhi proposes a reflective approach for Hindus, both within India and globally, in practising their faith. He und...

item.title: Elections BC Reveals BC United's Involvement in Website Criticizing MLA Rustad
item.description: Elections BC has determined that the B.C. United political party was secretly behind a website created to publicly call for the firing of MLA Trevor Rustad. The website, which employed an anonymous on...

item.title: Former DPWH Engineer Linked to P1.72 Billion in Flood Control Project Funds
item.description: A state witness in an ongoing corruption case has implicated former Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) engineer Brice Hernandez in an alleged scheme involving billions of pesos for flood c...

item.title: Unisa recognised for achievements in global subject rankings and national creative research outputs
item.description: On 25 March 2026, the QS World University Rankings by Subject revealed remarkable gains for the University of South Africa (Unisa), highlighting its expanding global profile across a range of academic...

item.title: PDEA seizes P3.4 million shabu from Malaysian and Filipino suspects in Tawi-Tawi
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) agents in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) successfully confiscated P3.4 million worth of shabu from two individual...

item.title: Transcorp Energy and Renewvia Partner to Develop Renewable Energy Projects Across Nigeria and Africa
item.description: Transcorp Energy Limited and Renewvia Solar Nigeria Limited have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to jointly develop renewable energy projects across Nigeria, with plans to expand their operations...
